records.ety: [F., from L. portio, akin to pars, partis, a part. See Part, n.]
usage: Portion, Part. Part is generic, having a simple reference to some whole. Portion has the additional idea of such a division as bears reference to an individual, or is allotted to some object; as, a portion of one's time; a portion of Scripture.

defs: That which is divided off or separated, as a part from a whole; a separated part of anything.
A part considered by itself, though not actually cut off or separated from the whole.
quotes: These are parts of his ways; but how little a portion is heard of him!
Portions and parcels of the dreadful past.

defs: A part assigned; allotment; share; fate.
quotes: The lord of that servant . . . will appoint him his portion with the unbelievers.
Man's portion is to die and rise again.

defs: The part of an estate given to a child or heir, or descending to him by law, and distributed to him in the settlement of the estate; an inheritance.
quotes: Give me the portion of goods that falleth to me.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Portioned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Portioning.]

defs: To separate or divide into portions or shares; to parcel; to distribute.
quotes: And portion to his tribes the wide domain.

defs: To endow with a portion or inheritance.
quotes: Him portioned maids, apprenticed orphans, blest.
